commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Matrix docs for existing `streaming: "progress"`, `streaming.progress` options, and `mentionPatterns` config in `docs/channels/matrix.md`.

PR surface: Docs +29. Total +29 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a docs-only PR, not a runtime bug. Source inspection confirms the documented fields exist, and the remaining check is same-page documentation consistency.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A repair worker can update the one stale Matrix configuration reference bullet on the contributor branch without a product decision.

target.title: docs(matrix): document missing streaming.progress mode, progress sub-fields, and mentionPatterns config
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Three config fields exist in the Matrix config schema (`extensions/matrix/src/config-schema.ts`) but had no documentation in `docs/channels/matrix.md`:

1. **`streaming: "progress"`** ŌĆö a valid streaming mode value accepted by the schema but missing from the streaming behavior table
2. **`streaming.progress`** object ŌĆö 5 sub-fields (`label`, `labels`, `maxLines`, `maxLineChars`, `toolProgress`) with no documentation
3. **`mentionPatterns`** ŌĆö a top-level channel config field completely absent from the 937-line doc

## Why This Change Was Made

Added documentation for three schema-valid but undocumented Matrix config fields. `streaming: "progress"` was missing from the streaming mode table despite being a first-class `z.enum` member. The `progress` sub-object (5 nested fields controlling progress-draft rendering) had no config example or field descriptions. `mentionPatterns` was absent from the access-policy reference section despite being read by `handler.ts:1127` to scope per-agent mention patterns to specific Matrix room IDs. All three additions are editorial-only ŌĆö the schema and runtime already support these fields.

## User Impact

Users can now discover three previously-valid-but-undocumented config features from the Matrix channel docs. No code behavior changes.

## Evidence

All three fields exist in the schema (`extensions/matrix/src/config-schema.ts`) and are consumed at runtime.

### `streaming: "progress"` mode ŌĆö schema + runtime

Schema at `config-schema.ts:84,130` includes `"progress"` in the enum:

```ts
z.enum(["partial", "quiet", "progress", "off"])
```

Runtime at `monitor/index.ts:85-87` resolves it:

```ts
if (streaming === "progress") {
  return "progress";
}
```

### `streaming.progress` sub-object ŌĆö schema

Schema at `config-schema.ts:85-94`:

```ts
progress: z.object({
  label: z.union([z.string(), z.literal(false)]).optional(),
  labels: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
  maxLines: z.number().int().positive().optional(),
  maxLineChars: z.number().int().positive().optional(),
  toolProgress: z.boolean().optional(),
}).strict().optional(),
```

UI hints at `config-ui-hints.ts:49-69` confirm field labels and descriptions.

### `mentionPatterns` ŌĆö schema + runtime

Schema at `config-schema.ts:126`:

```ts
mentionPatterns: MentionPatternsPolicySchema.optional(),
```

Runtime at `handler.ts:1124-1128` reads it as `providerPolicy` on the mention-regex builder:

```ts
const agentMentionRegexes = core.channel.mentions.buildMentionRegexes(cfg, _route.agentId, {
  provider: "matrix",
  conversationId: roomId,
  providerPolicy: accountConfig?.mentionPatterns,
});
```
target.author: wm0018
